PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar Nov-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
(Part 2 of 2 of mini minutes.)

No later than December 14, 2023, Plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than December 14, 2023, Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Defendants' affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown.

A further case management conference is set for January 25, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. There is no need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs are to make a settlement demand on Defendants no later than February 2, 2024. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 16, 2024. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Pang Ly via electronic mail by March 8, 2024 at 12:00 p.m. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, March 14, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: J. Alameda. Matter not reported (503)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Nov-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:
Michelle Golden of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant Parex USA, Inc.

RULING:

Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed.

The trial date is March 18, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is March 29, 2024. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 14, 2024. Such motions must be brought on regular notice under relevant provisions of the C.C.P. unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow only four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 1, 2024. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 15, 2024. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to Defendants no later than December 28, 2023. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Mini minutes continued, see next entry)
